steps.text: Routinely review published NPIs to ensure information accurately reflects the Agency’s current policies and procedures.
Update or archive outdated NPIs as appropriate.
Transmit and clear directives affecting field operations through all Regional Administrators.
Transmit all directives originating in the National Office to SOL after concurrence from all required clearance officials.

Clear regional directives by the Regional Administrator, Deputy Regional Administrator, affected Area Office Directors, National Office directorates and free-standing offices responsible for the functions covered, and the directives officer.
Transmit regional directives to the appropriate Regional Office of the Solicitor after concurrence from all required clearance officials.
Complete items 1 through 8 on the OSHA 201.
Determine whether field coordination is necessary.
Allow a minimum of 15 working days for review and clearance.

Prepare an electronic version of the OSHA 201 and a draft directive to provide electronically (e.g., email, shared folders, SharePoint, etc.) to all clearance officials and proxies, which may include administrative staff.
Identify all clearance officials and proxies who will receive a copy.
Obtain the approval of their Program Director and sends copies to the clearance officials and proxies.

Review and reconcile any comments noted in item 8 of the OSHA 201 by clearance officials/proxies in the National Office and in the field. Consult with clearance officials regarding comments, proposed policy and procedure changes, and proposed language.

Contact nonresponsive clearance offices prior to the review deadline to determine whether clearance/comments will be submitted by the requested deadline and offer an opportunity to discuss any questions or issues to expedite the process.

Refer to the Program Director any unresolved disagreements between the originator and clearance officials/proxies concerning the substance of the proposed policy issuance or the priority accorded to its review that has emerged during the clearance process.
Revise the draft directive and if necessary, complete an additional clearance round for concurrence.

Provide to the Office of the Solicitor, prior to submission to the directives officer, the draft directive for review/concurrence. Comments will be addressed, and additional clearance will be completed as necessary.

Prepare directive clearance packet for processing through the directives officer requesting approval and signature of the Assistant Secretary or the Agency Administrative Officer, as appropriate. Ensure the packet includes the final directive, talking points, signed OSHA 201s, comment and resolution document, and concurrence grid.
Provide the final unsigned 508-compliant PDF file to the directives officer for posting to the directives webpage.
Review, approve, and sign the OSHA 201 before distribution to the clearance officials.
Review comments and originator’s recommendations and approves the final draft directive.
Elevate unresolved issues as appropriate.
Escalate disputes with clearance officials to the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary for resolution.
Consult OAS for a shorter clearance time if an exception applies.
Contact the clearance official in question to negotiate a satisfactory extension for review or meet to resolve any issues, questions, or concerns.
Notify OAS of the extension request or other delays in clearance.
Schedule a meeting with the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary to resolve the disagreement on comments.
Elevate outstanding issues to the Assistant Secretary for a decision.
Provide union notification.
Submit final unsigned directives to the directives officer.
Submit publication requests to the web administrator.
Confirm the current directive template is being used.
Tentatively identify the type of directive and assign a subject following consultation with the directives officer.
Prepare a cancellation notice using the cancellation template on the OSHA Directives Tools webpage.
Obtain the approval of their Program Director.
Provide the cancellation notice and concurrence grid providing review by the originating directorate, the directives officer, and the OAS.
Provide the final unsigned 508-compliant PDF file to the directives officer for posting to the directives webpage.
Review the cancellation packet to determine if complete and accurate for the approval process.
Provide revision request from the OAS to the originator for necessary updates.
Provide notification of signed directive to the originator and request the cancellation notice be formatted to 508-compliant PDF.
Assign an effective date based on the approval by the Assistant Secretary.
Provide the final 508-compliant PDF file to the web administrator for posting to the OSHA Directives webpage.
Review 508-compliant PDF for web-related technical content.
Publish 508-compliant PDF to OSHA Directives webpage within requested
Notify the directives officer of availability on the OSHA Directives webpage.
Archive directives containing an expiration date.
Provide notification to national office directors and regional administrators of expiring directives for the upcoming fiscal year.
Maintain files in accordance with OSHA file retention policies.
Implement and comply with the provisions of this instruction and with related instructions governing the management of policy and non-policy issues.
Self-assess and routinely monitor the directive process using the checklist on the directives tools webpage.
Review and assess active directives on an annual basis and maintain a final document of the review findings in the national office or regional files.
Conduct a full assessment of the files for the directives approved that year and ensure all appropriate documents are included in the file.
Send an email or memo to the Director, Office of Audit, DAP, or designee documenting the completion of the requirement.
Provide documentation of completion to the directives officer.
Retain completion documentation in the directive file record.
Confirm the self-assessment documentation was received within the established deadline.
Include the auditing of the assessment process as part of audit responsibilities.
Refer to Appendix A for national clearance process.

preconditions.text: State Programs (DCSP) must approve appropriate language for all federal program change paragraphs.
Construction-related directives must have appropriate language approved by the Director of the Directorate of Construction (DOC).
Concurrence must be obtained from all required clearance officials.
If a shorter clearance period is desired, consult with the directives officer.
Prior to OAS submission, non-concurrence forms must have update to reflect concurrence.
A clearance office fails to return the OSHA 201 by the deadline
There is a failure to achieve an agreement on comments from clearance offices
There is an absence of resolution of the issue(s) from the Deputy Assistant Secretary
All final unsigned directives are 508 compliant

Directives may be canceled by reference in the cancellation paragraph of a new or revised instruction, notice, or direction, or by the issuance of a notice whose sole purpose is to cancel the directive.
It is quarter four of the current fiscal year
If making significant changes (major substantive changes, such as those that change the purpose or intent of the directive, or substantially alter OSHA policies and procedures)

constraints.text: Originators should be selective in clearing draft directives outside OSHA.
Organizations included in clearance must be those responsible for affected programs/standards/procedures, or administratively/legally responsible for reviewing the draft.
Chief executives of state and local governments or consultation organizations may comment on proposed directives which affect those programs.
Directions are a minimum of 5 working days for review and clearance.
The directives checklist may be used to ensure all requirements are met. The checklist is not required but recommended to help prevent delays.
Refer to or seek advice from the directives officer for current guidance as needed.
Union notification must be provided in accordance with Local 12 Collective Bargaining Agreement, Article 36 and NCFLL Collective Bargaining Agreement, Article 2.
The originating Program Director/Regional Administrator will not contact the union directly.
Communication must be between the Office of Employee and Labor Management Relations and the Office of Audit.
Required documentation includes: Final signed directive, All signed OSHA 201 forms, Record of comments and resolution, Draft documents, Talking points, and Concurrence grid.
Must be sent no later than September 30 of the fiscal year.
Must be confirmed in the first quarter of each fiscal year.
